title: helichrysum angustifolium subsp. microphyllum
intro: Helichrysum angustifolium subsp. microphyllum, also known as the alpine everlast, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Helichrysum angustifolium subsp. microphyllum can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used topically in creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
